explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.path_to_improvement: Document bias testing procedures or vendor AI fairness requirements.

governance.findings: Documentation details the establishment of a cross-functional AI task force that holds regular meetings to review use cases. It also outlines a policy for evaluating AI solutions, which aims to prevent shadow AI.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: This vendor case study provides evidence for the **governance**, **oversight**, **privacy**, and **transparency** pillars of responsible AI. It supports governance by detailing the establishment of a cross-functional AI task force with regular meetings to review use cases and a policy for evaluating AI solutions to prevent shadow AI. Oversight is demonstrated through an operational 'human-in-the-loop' model ensuring human review before AI actions, while privacy is touched upon via operational processes for vendor AI due diligence, including legal and security reviews and contractual agreements. The case study also implies transparency in the AI's operational processes.
relevant_sources.title: Torq Case Study: Hyper-AUTO-Mation: Why Carvana's CISO Bet on Agentic AI for 5x SOC Efficiency

oversight.findings: Documentation describes an operational 'human-in-the-loop' model. This model states that human review occurs before AI actions.
oversight.path_to_improvement: Publish override mechanisms, escalation processes, or appeal workflows.

privacy.findings: Documentation references operational processes for vendor AI due diligence. These processes specify the inclusion of legal and security reviews, as well as contractual agreements.
privacy.path_to_improvement: Publish AI-specific privacy impact assessments or data handling procedures.

transparency.findings: A case study references transparency in the AI's operational processes.
transparency.path_to_improvement: Publish detailed documentation (model cards, system specs) for AI systems deployed.

Oversight is demonstrated through an operational 'human-in-the-loop' model ensuring human review before AI actions, while privacy is touched upon via operational processes for vendor AI due diligence, including legal and security reviews and contractual agreements. summary.overall_findings: Drawing from 12 publicly available sources, Carvana's published materials address 4 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars. Operational practices include a 'human-in-the-loop' model with human review occurring before AI actions, as well as vendor AI due diligence processes that incorporate legal and security reviews. Further disclosures reference transparency in the AI's operational processes and detail the establishment of a cross-functional AI task force that holds regular meetings to review use cases. No qualifying public evidence was found for fairness, explainability, and 1 additional pillar.
